entities.name: tirzepatide

attributes.acylation_moiety: C20 fatty diacid via linker containing (2-[2-(2-Amino-ethoxy)-ethoxy]-acetyl)2-(gamma-Glu)1-CO-(CH2)18-CO2H
attributes.non_standard_amino_acids: Aib (2-aminoisobutyric acid) at positions X1 and X2

entities.context: The invention describes manufacturing processes and intermediates for producing tirzepatide, a GIP/GLP-1 dual agonist peptide.

entities.name: glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide receptor
entities.context: It activates both the glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) receptor and the glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor, with imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.

entities.name: glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor
entities.context: It activates both the glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) receptor and the glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor, with imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.

entities.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
entities.context: Tirzepatide is a dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist developed by Eli Lilly for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus and obesity.

entities.name: Obesity
entities.context: Tirzepatide is a dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist developed by Eli Lilly for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus and obesity.

entities.name: GIP/GLP-1 dual receptor agonism
entities.context: It activates both the glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) receptor and the glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor, with imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.

entities.name: SURPASS
entities.context: Clinical development includes the SURPASS and SURMOUNT trial programs demonstrating superior glycemic control and weight loss versus selective GLP-1 receptor agonists.

entities.name: SURMOUNT
entities.context: Clinical development includes the SURPASS and SURMOUNT trial programs demonstrating superior glycemic control and weight loss versus selective GLP-1 receptor agonists.

entities.name: selective GLP-1 receptor agonism

entities.name: solid-phase peptide synthesis
entities.context: The synthesis employs solid-phase peptide synthesis (SPPS) using Fmoc chemistry with multiple peptide intermediates (Preparations 1-42) using various protecting groups and configurations.
